steps.title: Identify the sentence elements
steps.content: The blank is the main verb of the sentence. The subject is “nearly 30% of the city's population” (a singular noun phrase). The sentence includes a time reference phrase, “Even back in the 1860s,” at the beginning.

steps.title: Apply the tense rule
steps.content: The rule is that a verb must use a tense matching the sentence's time reference. The past reference (1860s) requires a past-tense verb. For the singular subject, that's “was.” All other choices use present or future tense, contradicting the 1860s time marker. That matches choice B — B is correct.
